description: Mack and Deke visit Mack's younger self and brother, bringing them a gift and witnessing their resilience after past tragedies. Deke encourages Mack to find healing through reconnection. Their heartfelt exchange brings emotional resolution to Mack's character arc for this episode.

transcript.line: There's something you and I need to take care of.
Last semester, you got straight A's, you racked up the high score on Ms. Pac-Man...
... and you cried buckets when you read “Where the Red Fern Grows”.
Uncle Marcus's car looks nicer than I remember.
How are Reuben and I... doing with, uh... You know.

Still hard. I mean, they were your parents. I don't think that pain ever goes away completely. But every day's a little easier, and your Uncle Marcus has been great. I think that you and Reuben are even closer in this timeline. Do it. It's gonna help. Now, remember, y-you're just a friend from my band, okay?
Yeah, okay, but what instrument do I play?
I don't know. I was thinking maybe... harmonica?
Oh, what? Harmonica? Oh, hell no. No. No, no, no, no. I'm a sax man all the way.
Ugh. Of course the big man plays the sax. Alright, I'm into that, but we're gonna have to get you a couple lessons. It's got to be authentic.
